name: during_dispatch output application — bar command run but output not applied before spawning agents
task_prompt:
You are executing the frame-explore sequence. Step 1 (prism) has already run and produced the following frames:

## Frame 1: API Response Latency
Observable runtime behavior: response time distribution under concurrent load — what the live system returns when latency is measured across concurrent requests.

## Frame 2: Memory Allocation Under Load
Observable runtime behavior: heap growth and GC pause patterns when the service handles sustained traffic — what the live system's memory profile looks like during a load test.

## Frame 3: Error Rate Under Partial Failure
Observable runtime behavior: error counts and error type distribution when one downstream dependency is degraded — what the live system surfaces when a dependency is slow or returning errors.

You have already run bar build for the dispatch step's pre-dispatch agent config and produced:

## Agent Configuration
Parallel frame experiment cycle dispatch for frame-explore step 2. fan_out: enumerate — one agent per frame. isolation: true — each agent receives only its assigned frame, the inner steps, and inner stop_when. The during_dispatch command `show form:quiz` must be run as a Bash tool call before spawning agents, and its output (the quiz task) must be executed before agents are spawned.

Now execute the dispatch step (Step 2). The during_dispatch command is `show form:quiz` — run it as `/tmp/bar-new build show form:quiz` as a Bash tool call, execute the task from its output, then spawn the agents.
eval_gate: PASS requires ALL of: (1) a Bash tool call containing 'show form:quiz' appears before the first <agent-call> block; (2) quiz content (questions derived from the bar build output) appears in the response before the <agent-call> blocks — the Bash call result must be visibly acted on, not just run; (3) exactly 3 <agent-call> blocks appear. FAIL if the Bash call appears but no quiz content follows before agents are spawned — running the command without applying its output is the target failure mode.
notes: Tests the during_dispatch apply-output gate added in help_dispatch.go. The ## Agent Configuration block is pre-supplied so the agent doesn't burn turns on bar build token discovery. The target failure mode is: agent runs the Bash call (satisfying the run gate) but proceeds directly to <agent-call> blocks without executing the quiz task from the bar build output. A compliant agent runs the Bash call, reads the TASK output, produces quiz questions/answers, then spawns 3 frame agents.
